skill.name: onboarding-cro
skill.description: Helps teams improve post-signup onboarding, activation, first-run experience, empty states, onboarding checklists, and time-to-value.
skill.summary: Optimize user onboarding flows to increase activation and early retention.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context and dismissed as false positives. The backtick detections are Markdown code fences, weak cryptography detections are ordinary product words such as signup and design, and the reconnaissance detection is a related-skill reference. No prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, secret access, or command execution intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown fenced examples for checklist and funnel formatting. No executable command, shell invocation, or user-input command construction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact locations contain only triple backtick Markdown fences around plain text examples. There is no code execution syntax or runtime context in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged weak cryptography locations are natural-language onboarding content, including the skill description and references to design. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms appear inside product and UX prose, not cryptographic implementation. The duplicate line 66 alert maps to the phrase design tool, which has no security-sensitive behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged line is a related-skill pointer to paywall-upgrade-cro. It does not request host details, environment data, file listings, network discovery, or system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a Related Skills list and contains no instruction to inspect the runtime or host system. No surrounding context changes that interpretation.

content.value_statement: Many products lose users before they reach clear value. This skill helps diagnose onboarding gaps and design activation-focused flows, copy, metrics, and experiments.
actual_capabilities: Audits post-signup onboarding flows for friction, unclear next steps, and delayed value.
Defines activation goals and aha moments based on product type and retention signals.
Designs onboarding checklists, guided setup flows, empty states, and milestone messages.
Plans activation metrics, funnel analysis, cohort tracking, and experiment ideas.
Drafts welcome screen copy, tooltip copy, onboarding email copy, and recovery messages.
limitations: Does not replace analytics implementation or statistically valid experiment analysis.
Requires product context, funnel data, or user behavior details for precise recommendations.
Focuses on post-signup onboarding, not signup form optimization.
Does not create production UI code or automate changes in product systems.

use_cases.title: Improve SaaS Activation
use_cases.description: Audit the first session, define the activation event, and redesign setup steps for faster value.

use_cases.title: Fix Empty States
use_cases.description: Turn blank dashboards and unused areas into clear prompts that guide users toward useful actions.

use_cases.title: Plan Retention Experiments
use_cases.description: Create focused onboarding tests for checklists, tours, progress indicators, emails, and support prompts.

prompt_templates.title: Audit My Onboarding Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing post-signup flow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my post-signup onboarding flow. Ask for any missing context, then identify friction, unclear steps, delayed value, and priority fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Define Activation Metrics
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate activation planning for a product team.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help define the activation event for my product. Use product type, user goal, retention signals, and current funnel data to recommend metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Design a First-Run Experience
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate flow design for a new or redesigned product.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a first-run onboarding experience for my product. Include the activation goal, steps, empty states, checklist items, copy, and measurement plan.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Experiment Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced growth roadmap for improving activation and retention.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an onboarding CRO experiment roadmap. Prioritize tests by expected activation impact, implementation effort, measurement clarity, and learning value.

output_examples.input: Audit a project management app where new users land on an empty dashboard after signup.
output: Finding: The empty dashboard delays the first useful action.
Impact: Users may not understand how to create value in the product.
Recommendation: Add a guided first project template and one primary create-project action.

output_examples.input: Design onboarding for an analytics product that needs tracking installed before value appears.
output: Activation goal: User sees the first meaningful report from real or sample data.
Step one: Choose platform and copy tracking instructions.
Step two: Show validation status and a sample report while data arrives.
Metrics plan: Track install completion, report view rate, and time to first report.

best_practices: Start with the activation event and work backward to the shortest path.
Use real user behavior data when prioritizing onboarding changes.
Give every onboarding screen one clear primary action.
anti_patterns: Teaching every feature before the user experiences value.
Blocking product access behind long setup steps without a clear reason.
Using tooltips or tours to compensate for confusing core navigation.

faq.question: What part of onboarding does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers the post-signup experience, including activation, first-run flows, empty states, checklists, messages, and metrics.

faq.question: Can it help define an activation event?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides teams to identify the earliest behavior that predicts retention and product value.

faq.question: Does it optimize signup forms?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on what happens after signup. Signup form optimization belongs in a separate signup flow review.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide product type, user goal, current onboarding steps, activation rate, drop-off points, and any cohort data.

faq.question: Can it write onboarding copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft welcome screens, checklist text, tooltips, empty states, emails, and milestone messages.

faq.question: Can it validate experiment results?
faq.answer: It can suggest what to measure, but it does not replace statistical analysis or analytics tooling.
